page: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-112 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance designed for memory-intensive enterprise workloads. Built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, this instance provides 112 dedicated virtual CPUs paired with 1488.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a high memory-to-core ratio of approximately 13.29 GB of RAM per vCPU, ensuring ample memory capacity for large-scale datasets.

This instance does not include local NVMe SSD storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external block storage options. Additionally, the m4-megamem-112 does not feature integrated GPU hardware accelerators, making it unsuitable for workloads that require hardware-accelerated graphics or GPU-based machine learning. The instance also includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, requiring external networking configurations for public internet access.

Given its massive memory footprint and high dedicated vCPU count, the m4-megamem-112 is engineered for large in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and real-time data analytics. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile represents a specialized resource allocation where the investment is heavily weighted toward memory density rather than local storage or acceleration hardware, making it highly efficient for workloads that bottleneck on RAM capacity rather than disk I/O or parallel GPU processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-112 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance built on the x86_64 architecture, featuring 112 dedicated vCPUs and 1488.0 GB of RAM. This configuration delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 13.29 GB per vCPU, making it highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise applications. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, meaning all storage needs must be handled via network-attached volumes. It is designed specifically for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and complex data analytics workloads that require massive memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or hardware acceleration.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform m4-megamem-112 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 112 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 1488 GB of RAM, designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated compute cores and massive RAM capacity for enterprise-scale in-memory workloads.

Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases and ERP systems
